title: Full SEO Optimization: Meta Tags, Open Graph, Sitemap, Robots.txt, Schema.org Structured Data

requirements.text: Add per-page SEO meta tags to every route in the React SPA. Each page must have a unique <title> tag and <meta name='description'> tag. Use a React-based head management approach (e.g., a reusable SEOHead component using document.title + meta tag injection or react-helmet-async). Pages and their suggested titles/descriptions: Home — 'Coal & Curry | Authentic South Indian Charcoal Restaurant in Neyveli'; About — 'About Us | Coal & Curry – Chettinad Charcoal Cooking, Neyveli'; Menu — 'Menu | Coal & Curry – South Indian Charcoal Grilled Dishes, Biryani & More'; Offers — 'Special Offers | Coal & Curry Restaurant Neyveli'; Gallery — 'Gallery | Coal & Curry – Charcoal Grill & Restaurant Ambience'; Contact — 'Contact Us | Coal & Curry, Neyveli Tamil Nadu'; Reservation — 'Reserve a Table | Coal & Curry Restaurant Neyveli'; Blog — 'Blog | Coal & Curry – Food Stories & Recipes'; Loyalty — 'Loyalty Program | Coal & Curry Neyveli'; Cart — 'Your Cart | Coal & Curry'; Checkout — 'Checkout | Coal & Curry'.

quotes: Add full SEO optimization to the site so it has the best chance of appearing in Google searches for terms like 'Coal & Curry Neyveli' or 'best restaurant in Neyveli'
proper meta title and description tags on every page

acceptanceCriteria: Every page route renders a unique <title> in the document head
Every page route renders a <meta name='description'> with a relevant description
Titles include the restaurant name and location keywords (Coal & Curry, Neyveli)
A reusable SEOHead component or equivalent is used consistently across all page components

requirements.text: Add Open Graph (OG) and Twitter Card meta tags to all pages so shared links on social media render rich previews. Each page should include: og:title, og:description, og:type ('website' for most, 'restaurant' for Home), og:url (relative canonical path), og:image (pointing to the restaurant logo or a hero food image from /assets/generated), og:site_name ('Coal & Curry'), og:locale ('en_IN'), twitter:card ('summary_large_image'), twitter:title, twitter:description, and twitter:image.

quotes: Open Graph tags for social sharing

acceptanceCriteria: All 11 page routes include og:title, og:description, og:image, og:type, og:site_name, og:locale meta tags
All 11 page routes include twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image meta tags
og:image references a valid static asset path from /assets/generated
og:site_name is set to 'Coal & Curry' on all pages

requirements.text: Create a static sitemap.xml file at frontend/public/sitemap.xml listing all public routes of the application: /, /menu, /about, /offers, /gallery, /contact, /reservation, /blog, /loyalty. Each URL entry should include a <loc> with the canonical base URL placeholder (e.g., https://coalandcurry.com) appended with the path, a <lastmod> set to today's date, and a <changefreq> appropriate per page (home: 'weekly', menu/offers: 'weekly', about/contact/loyalty: 'monthly', gallery/blog: 'weekly').

quotes: a sitemap.xml helps Google understand your site structure

acceptanceCriteria: frontend/public/sitemap.xml exists and is valid XML
All 9 main routes are listed as <url> entries
Each entry has <loc>, <lastmod>, and <changefreq> child elements
The file is accessible at /sitemap.xml when the app is served

requirements.text: Create a robots.txt file at frontend/public/robots.txt that allows all crawlers (User-agent: *) to index the site, disallows /cart and /checkout paths (since they should not be indexed), and includes a Sitemap reference pointing to /sitemap.xml.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/public/robots.txt exists
Contains 'User-agent: *' with 'Allow: /'
Disallows /cart and /checkout
Includes 'Sitemap: /sitemap.xml' directive
File is accessible at /robots.txt when the app is served

requirements.text: Add Schema.org JSON-LD structured data of type 'Restaurant' to the Home page (and optionally embedded in the index.html head for global coverage). The structured data must include: @type: 'Restaurant', name: 'Coal & Curry', description: 'Authentic South Indian charcoal-grilled cuisine in Neyveli, Tamil Nadu', url: 'https://coalandcurry.com', address (@type: PostalAddress) with streetAddress, addressLocality: 'Neyveli', addressRegion: 'Tamil Nadu', addressCountry: 'IN', telephone: restaurant phone number placeholder, servesCuisine: ['South Indian', 'Chettinad', 'Tamil'], priceRange: '₹₹', openingHours: appropriate hours string (e.g., 'Mo-Su 11:00-23:00'), hasMenu: '/menu', and image pointing to the restaurant logo asset. Inject this as a <script type='application/ld+json'> tag in the document head on the Home page.

quotes: Schema.org structured data (Restaurant type) with your restaurant name, location in Neyveli, cuisine type, and other rich result details
helps Google show rich results like address, hours, and star ratings

acceptanceCriteria: Home page renders a <script type='application/ld+json'> tag with valid Restaurant Schema.org JSON-LD
Structured data includes name, description, address (Neyveli, Tamil Nadu), servesCuisine, priceRange, openingHours, and image fields
JSON-LD is valid and parseable
Google Rich Results Test would recognize the Restaurant entity from this markup

constraints: Do not modify any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths
All SEO tags must be injected client-side using a safe React-compatible approach; do not alter index.html structure beyond adding static meta defaults
sitemap.xml and robots.txt must be placed in frontend/public so they are served at the root path
nonGoals: Google Search Console integration or submission (requires manual user action)
Dynamic server-side rendering or SSR for SEO
Paid SEO tools or third-party analytics integration
Changing any existing page layouts or visual design
